menu.title: EU Puzzle
menu.subtitle: Rebuild the map of the European Union

confirm.discardMsg: You have already connected {count} countries. Are you sure you want to restart?
confirm.tutorialMsg: You have already connected {count} countries. Starting the tutorial will reset the game.
confirm.menuMsg: You have connected {count} countries. Going back to the menu will lose your progress.

welcome.title: European Union Puzzle
welcome.subtitle: Rebuild the EU map by connecting all 27 member countries!

welcome.title: Welcome!
welcome.text: You are here to rebuild the map of the European Union. The 27 countries are scattered and your goal is to put them back in their place! This tutorial will guide you step by step.

board.title: The Game Area
board.text: This is the playing area. The puzzle pieces are scattered around here. Depending on your difficulty, you will need to drag them to their correct spot or snap them together.

snapEasy.title: Drag to the Outline
snapEasy.text: Drag France to its matching outline on the board. The piece will automatically snap into place when you get close enough!

snapMedium.title: Snap the Borders
snapMedium.text: Snap France to Germany. Bring the two countries closer until they join automatically. Golden arrows will show you the direction!

rotateHard.title: Rotate & Snap
rotateHard.text: In Hard mode, pieces are rotated. Right-click or scroll with your mouse over France to rotate it until it's upright, then snap it to Germany!

info.title: Info and Curiosities
info.text: Now that you have placed the piece, click the map pin to discover real information and facts! Note: when you connect countries this tab will open automatically.

progress.title: Progress Bar
progress.text: Here you see how many countries you have already connected. The goal is to bring the counter to 27 out of 27!

settings.title: Settings
settings.text: From the Settings button you can adjust the volume of the music and sound effects, and change the text size for better readability.

reopen.title: Reopen the Tutorial
reopen.text: Have doubts during the game? Press the 'Tutorial' button at the top of the page to reopen this guide at any time!

hint.title: Hint
hint.text: Are you stuck? This button highlights the smallest country not yet connected. Use it sparingly so you don't lose the taste for the challenge!

reset.title: Restart
reset.text: If you want to start over, use the Reset button. All pieces are reshuffled and you can try again at your leisure!

ready.title: You're Ready!
ready.text: Now you know everything you need. Good luck rebuilding the European Union! Remember: you can always reopen this tutorial from the button at the top. You can do it!

error.loadGame: Error loading the game. Please reload the page.

040.name: Austria
facts: Vienna hosts the oldest amusement park in the world still in operation: the Prater.
62% of Austrian territory is covered by the Alps.
The sewing machine was invented by Austrian Josef Madersperger.
Vienna is home to the world's oldest zoo (Tiergarten Schönbrunn).
The founder of Porsche, Ferdinand Porsche, was Austrian.
Austria is not a NATO member and is constitutionally neutral.
The national flag is one of the oldest in the world (1191).
Red Bull has Austrian origins.
Postcards were invented in Austria.
Mozart was born in Salzburg, Austria.

056.name: Belgium
facts: Belgium produces 220,000 tonnes of chocolate per year.
Brussels Airport sells more chocolate than any other place in the world.
The saxophone was invented by Adolphe Sax in Belgium.
Belgium holds the world record for the longest period without a government (541 days).
It is home to Tomorrowland, one of the largest music festivals in the world.
French fries are said to have been invented here.
Belgium has the highest number of castles per square kilometre.
Billiard balls were invented in Belgium.
The Smurfs were created by Belgian artist Peyo.
Brussels is considered the capital of the European Union.

100.name: Bulgaria
facts: In Bulgaria, shaking your head means 'yes' and nodding means 'no'.
The oldest worked gold treasure in the world was found at Varna.
Bulgaria produces 85% of the world's rose oil.
The Cyrillic alphabet originated here.
The yogurt bacterium (Lactobacillus bulgaricus) is only found naturally here.
Plovdiv is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in Europe.
The bagpipe (Gaida) is a national instrument.
Bulgaria is the only European country that has never changed its name since 681 AD.
Computer pioneer John Atanasoff had Bulgarian roots.
Nearly a third of the country is covered by forests.

191.name: Croatia
facts: The modern necktie derives from Croatia (from 17th-century Croatian soldiers).
Game of Thrones' King's Landing was filmed in Dubrovnik.
Nikola Tesla was born in Smiljan, Croatia.
Croatia has 1,246 islands, islets and reefs.
The Dalmatian dog breed is named after the Dalmatia region.
Croatia is home to the world's smallest town, Hum.
Stone from the island of Brač was used in the White House.
Croatia has the richest collection of Neanderthal remains in the world.
The torpedo was invented in Rijeka.
Zadar has a sea organ that plays music using ocean waves.

196.name: Cyprus
facts: Geographically in Asia but culturally and politically European.
The goddess Aphrodite is said to have been born here.
Commandaria is the world's oldest wine still in production.
Nicosia is the only divided capital in the world.
Cyprus is famous for Halloumi cheese.
There are more cats than humans on the island.
Cyprus enjoys around 340 days of sunshine per year.
It is the third largest island in the Mediterranean.
The name of copper (Cuprum) derives from Cyprus.
Cyprus is home to the mouflon, a rare species of wild sheep.

203.name: Czech Republic
facts: Czechs are the world's largest beer consumers per capita.
Prague is known as the City of a Hundred Spires.
Soft contact lenses were invented by a Czech chemist.
The word 'Robot' was coined by Czech writer Karel Čapek.
Prague Castle is the largest ancient castle complex in the world.
Sugar cubes were invented in the Czech Republic.
The country is famous for Bohemian crystal.
Charles University is one of the oldest in Europe (1348).
Škoda is one of the oldest car manufacturers.
The country is almost entirely surrounded by mountains.

208.name: Denmark
facts: The Danish flag is the oldest still in use in the world (since 1219).
LEGO was invented in Denmark.
No point in the country is more than 52 km from the sea.
Nine out of ten people in Denmark own a bicycle.
The concept of 'Hygge' (cosiness and comfort) was born here.
Denmark has Europe's oldest monarchy.
Hans Christian Andersen (The Little Mermaid) was Danish.
The Tivoli Gardens inspired Walt Disney.
Greenland is an autonomous territory of Denmark.
Bluetooth technology is named after King Harald Bluetooth.

233.name: Estonia
facts: Estonia was the first country to allow online voting.
Skype was developed by Estonian programmers.
50% of the country is covered by forests.
Nearly all public services are digitised (e-Estonia).
Estonia has over 2,000 islands.
Wife carrying is a popular sport that originated in the region.
Public transport in Tallinn is free for residents.
Estonia is one of the least religious countries in the world.
The literacy rate is 99.8%.
Kiiking (extreme swinging) is a sport invented here.

246.name: Finland
facts: There are more saunas than cars in Finland (3.3 million saunas).
Rovaniemi is the official home of Santa Claus.
Finland is ranked as the world's happiest country.
Finland has the highest per capita milk consumption in the world.
Finland hosts the World Air Guitar Championships.
It is called the land of 188,000 lakes.
Finland was the first European country to give women the vote (1906).
Angry Birds was created in Finland.
The Northern Lights can be seen in Lapland for 200 nights a year.
Finnish sauna culture is a UNESCO intangible cultural heritage.

250.name: France
facts: France is the most visited country in the world.
The Lumière brothers invented cinema here.
France produces over 1,600 types of cheese.
The Louvre is the largest art museum in the world.
French was the official language of England for 300 years.
The hot air balloon was invented in France.
It is legal to marry a deceased person in France (in special cases).
France hosts the Tour de France, the most famous cycling race.
Champagne can only be produced in the Champagne region.
The metric system was invented during the French Revolution.

276.name: Germany
facts: There are over 1,500 types of German sausages.
Germany was the first country to adopt daylight saving time.
The Christmas tree tradition originated in Germany.
Germany has over 20,000 castles.
Berlin has more bridges than Venice.
Germany is the largest economy in the European Union.
Large sections of the Autobahn have no speed limit.
X-rays were discovered by German physicist Röntgen.
Beer purity laws in Germany date back to 1516.
Movable-type printing was invented by Gutenberg in Germany.

300.name: Greece
facts: Greece has more archaeological museums than any other country.
Greece is the birthplace of democracy.
The Olympic Games originated in Greece.
Greece has thousands of islands, but only about 200 are inhabited.
Voting is legally mandatory for all adult citizens.
The Greek language has been spoken for over 3,000 years.
80% of the Greek territory is mountainous.
Feta cheese is a nationally protected product.
Greece enjoys sunshine for 250 days a year.
The word 'alphabet' comes from the first two Greek letters, Alpha and Beta.

348.name: Hungary
facts: Hungary has won more Olympic gold medals per capita than any country that has never hosted the Games.
The Rubik's Cube was invented by Hungarian Ernő Rubik.
Budapest has the most thermal springs of any capital city in the world.
The Hungarian language is unique and non-Indo-European.
Lake Balaton is the largest lake in Central Europe.
Hungary has the second oldest metro in Europe (after London).
The ballpoint pen (biro) was invented by a Hungarian.
Vitamin C was discovered by Hungarian scientist Albert Szent-Györgyi.
The Budapest Parliament is the third largest parliament building in the world.
Paprika is the national spice of Hungary.

372.name: Ireland
facts: The harp is the national symbol — Ireland is the only country with a musical instrument as its symbol.
Halloween derives from the ancient Celtic festival of Samhain.
Saint Patrick was not Irish; he was Romano-British.
The Titanic was built in Belfast on the island of Ireland.
The Guinness brewery has a 9,000-year lease.
The Wild Atlantic Way is the longest coastal road in the world.
More people of Irish descent live abroad than in Ireland itself.
Ireland has won the Eurovision Song Contest 7 times.
There are no snakes in Ireland.
The Obama and Kennedy families have Irish roots.

380.name: Italy
facts: Italy has the highest number of UNESCO World Heritage Sites (59).
Italy has two independent states within its borders: Vatican City and San Marino.
Modern pizza was born in Naples.
The University of Bologna is the oldest in Europe (1088).
Every year 500,000 people visit the Pope.
There is a fountain that distributes free wine 24/7 in Abruzzo.
Italians invented the piano, the thermometer and electric batteries.
Rome has over 2,700 years of history.
Italy has three active volcanoes: Etna, Stromboli and Vesuvius.
Christopher Columbus was Italian.

428.name: Latvia
facts: Riga has the largest collection of Art Nouveau buildings in the world.
Latvia is one of the greenest countries, with 50% forests.
Jeans were invented by Latvian tailor Jacob Davis.
Amber is considered the national gemstone of Latvia.
Latvia has one of the fastest internet speeds in the world.
The Ventas Rumba waterfall is the widest in Europe.
Riga's central market is housed in old Zeppelin hangars.
Latvia claims to have had the first Christmas tree in history (1510).
Ice hockey is the national sport of Latvia.
Latvia has a collection of over 1.5 million folk songs.

440.name: Lithuania
facts: Lithuanian is the oldest Indo-European language still spoken.
Basketball is considered a second religion in Lithuania.
There is an official perfume called 'Scent of Lithuania'.
The geographical centre of Europe is located here according to some calculations.
The Hill of Crosses hosts over 100,000 crosses.
In the 14th century Lithuania was the largest country in Europe.
The stork is the national bird and greatly loved.
At Easter it is 'Grandma Easter' who brings eggs, not the Easter bunny.
Fried garlic bread is the most popular snack in Lithuania.
Lithuania has one of the fastest public internet connections in the world.

442.name: Luxembourg
facts: Luxembourg has the highest GDP per capita in the world.
It was the first country to make all public transport free.
Luxembourg is the only remaining Grand Duchy in the world.
The national motto is 'We want to remain what we are'.
Luxembourg has three official languages: French, German and Luxembourgish.
Nearly 50% of the population is foreign-born.
Luxembourg City is a UNESCO World Heritage Site for its fortifications.
Skype has its European headquarters in Luxembourg.
Prime Minister Xavier Bettel was the first openly gay EU leader to marry in office.
Restaurant Chiggeri in Luxembourg has the longest wine list in the world.

470.name: Malta
facts: Malta has been a filming location for Gladiator, Troy and Game of Thrones.
Malta welcomes 3 times more tourists than its own population each year.
Traffic in Malta drives on the left, a British heritage.
The island of Comino has only 3 permanent residents.
Maltese is the only Semitic language written in Latin characters.
Malta has 365 churches, one for every day of the year.
Valletta is one of the smallest capital cities in Europe.
Malta's megalithic temples are older than Stonehenge.
The British George Cross is depicted on the Maltese flag.
Malta enjoys over 300 days of sunshine per year.

528.name: Netherlands
facts: 26% of the Netherlands lies below sea level.
There are more bicycles (22 million) than people (17 million) in the Netherlands.
Dutch men are the tallest in the world on average.
Amsterdam is built on millions of wooden piles.
Carrots are orange in honour of the Dutch Royal House of Orange.
The Netherlands is the world's largest exporter of flowers, especially tulips.
Schiphol Airport is located 4 metres below sea level.
The Dutch love salty liquorice, known as drop.
The Netherlands is home to major companies like Philips and Shell.
The Dutch national anthem (Wilhelmus) is the oldest in the world.

616.name: Poland
facts: Poland has 17 Nobel Prize winners in its history.
Marie Curie (born Skłodowska) was Polish.
Poland adopted the first constitution in Europe (1791).
Malbork Castle is the largest in the world by surface area.
The origin of vodka is disputed between Poland and Russia.
Pope John Paul II was Polish.
Poland disappeared from maps for 123 years.
The Białowieża Forest is home to the last wild European bison.
Poland is one of the largest exporters of amber.
It is common in Poland to eat pizza with ketchup.

620.name: Portugal
facts: Portugal has the oldest national borders in Europe (defined in 1143).
Portugal produces 50% of the world's cork.
The Vasco da Gama Bridge is the longest in Europe.
Port wine takes its name from the city of Porto.
Portuguese explorers Magellan and Vasco da Gama mapped the world.
Portugal is home to the world's oldest bookshop (Bertrand, Lisbon).
Painted tiles called Azulejos are a national symbol of Portugal.
Fado music is a UNESCO intangible cultural heritage.
Japanese tempura derives from a Portuguese dish.
Nazaré has some of the world's biggest waves for surfing.

642.name: Romania
facts: The Palace of Parliament is the heaviest administrative building in the world.
Romania is the homeland of the Dracula legend (Vlad the Impaler).
Romania has one of the fastest 4G speeds in Europe.
Romania has the largest brown bear population in Europe, excluding Russia.
Timișoara was the first European city with electric street lighting.
Famous sculptor Constantin Brâncuși was Romanian.
The Danube Delta is a UNESCO biosphere reserve.
Romanian is a Romance language, similar to Italian.
The Merry Cemetery of Săpânța is famous for its colourful crosses.
Romania has a rich tradition of folk costumes and crafts.

703.name: Slovakia
facts: Slovakia has the highest number of castles per capita in the world.
Slovakia has over 6,000 caves.
Bratislava is the only capital that borders two countries: Austria and Hungary.
The geographical centre of Europe is located in Slovakia according to one measurement.
Vlkolínec is a UNESCO World Heritage village of folk architecture.
Slovakia produces the most cars per capita in the world.
The Tatra Mountains are the smallest alpine mountains in Europe.
The national dish is potato dumplings with sheep's cheese.
The flying car AeroMobil was developed in Slovakia.
Slovakia's flag is very similar to those of Slovenia and Russia.

705.name: Slovenia
facts: Slovenia is one of the greenest countries in the world, with 60% forests.
Beekeeping is a major tradition in Slovenia (World Bee Day was inspired here).
The Postojna Caves are home to the 'proteus', an amphibian resembling a dragon.
The first married couple to climb Everest was Slovenian.
There is one vineyard for every 70 inhabitants in Slovenia.
Mount Triglav is the national symbol depicted on the flag.
Slovenia has one of the largest bear populations in Europe.
Lake Bled with its island is a world-famous icon.
Melania Trump is originally from Slovenia.
Skiing is the national sport of Slovenia.

724.name: Spain
facts: Spain has more bars per inhabitant than any other EU country.
Spain was the first global empire on which 'the sun never set'.
Spain produces 43% of the world's olive oil.
The Sagrada Família has been under construction since 1882.
Spanish is the second most spoken native language in the world.
Spain is the only European country that produces bananas, in the Canary Islands.
Spain is famous for the Running of the Bulls in Pamplona.
Spain is home to the world's oldest restaurant, Botín, opened in 1725.
The mop was a Spanish invention.
Spain is a world leader in organ donation.

752.name: Sweden
facts: Sweden has not been at war for over 200 years (since 1814).
Sweden imports waste from other countries to power its incinerators.
The pop group ABBA comes from Sweden.
Sweden is home to IKEA, Volvo and H&M.
Spotify and Skype were both born in Sweden.
Moose accidents are a real hazard on Swedish roads.
Nobel Prizes are awarded in Sweden (except the Peace Prize).
Fika (coffee break) is a sacred social institution in Sweden.
Sweden has 267,570 islands, more than any other country.
Sweden recycles 99% of household waste.
